publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-10-14 23:09:17 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Correspondence, course outlines, trip reports, questionnaires, memoranda and other papers deriving from Dr. Wylie's professional work in child development and family studies, including twin studies and the Cornell child study clubs; includes some personal correspondence, as with Flora Rose.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Child Development and Family Relationships, Cornell University. Dr. Wylie established the extension program in Family Life.

Margaret Wylie earned her bachelor's, master's, and doctorate degrees from the University of Michigan. She arrived at the College of Home Economics in 1925 and for three years she initiated extension institutes and follow-up programs in eight counties in New York State. She left Cornell to complete her Ph.D. in psychology and retuned in 1930 and remained until 1957. She was responsible for developing child study clubs and establishing Cornell's extension program in family life.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, course outlines, trip reports, questionnaires, memoranda and other papers deriving from Dr. Wylie's professional work in child development and family studies, including twin studies and the Cornell child study clubs; includes some personal correspondence, as with Flora Rose.

emph.ref: Series II. Subject Files, Correspondence, Booklets, and Questionnaires

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Margaret Wylie papers, #23-13-491. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Child development -- Study and teaching.
Families -- Study and teaching.
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Home Economics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1931 letter about the birth of daughter Patsy Ann and a 1934 photograph of her at the nursery school.
